message:
You know that one friend who always seems to panic at the slightest hint of trouble? Yep, that’s me! Just last week, we were having a cozy dinner when the lights flickered. My heart raced, and I immediately started imagining worst-case scenarios. My partner just chuckled and said, "How about we just check the fuse box first?" 

After I calmed down, I realized that being prepared doesn’t mean you have to be on high alert all the time. It’s about having a plan in place and knowing where your go-bag is stashed. A little preparedness can turn those panic moments into minor inconveniences. 

Anyone else have a friend who tends to jump to conclusions? What’s your go-to way of staying calm in those moments? By the way, I found this little refresher helpful!
